Merkel Nursing Center

CCN: 676053 · Merkel, TX 79536 · Taylor County

Overview

Address
1704 N 1ST, Merkel, TX 79536
Phone
3259285673
Certified beds
65
Avg daily residents
27 (42% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2005-06-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.78 3.21 4.55 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.44 0.56 0.53 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.31 0.71 1.57 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.04 1.94 2.45 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.75 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.20
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.34
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.85
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
60.5%
annual
RN turnover
40.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.14
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.83
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-12-04 21 13 8 301 1 301
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-31 35 19 35 216 1 216
Total weighted health score
279.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (56)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

7 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
48 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0940 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program for all new and existing staff members. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0941 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program that includes effective communications for direct care staff members. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0942 Ensure that staff members are educated on resident rights and facility responsibilities to properly care for its residents. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0945 Include as part of its infection prevention and control program, mandatory training that includes written standards, policies, and procedures for the program. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. E 2025-12-04 2026-01-07
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2025-08-22 2025-09-17
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2025-08-22 2025-09-17
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2025-08-22 2025-09-17
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. K 2025-08-22 2025-09-01
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2025-08-22 2025-09-25
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2025-08-22 2025-09-15
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2025-08-22 2025-09-16
0844 Follow rules about disclosure of ownership requirements and tell the state agency about changes in ownership and/or administrative personnel. C 2025-08-22 2025-09-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-01
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. E 2024-10-17 2024-11-08
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-10-17 2024-11-08
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-10-17 2024-11-08
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-10-17 2024-11-08
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. E 2024-10-17 2024-11-08
0926 Have policies on smoking. E 2024-10-17 2024-11-08
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2024-07-31 2024-09-01
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-07-31 2024-09-01
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. C 2024-07-31 2024-08-31
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-07-31 2024-09-15
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-07-31 2024-09-01
0680 Ensure the activities program is directed by a qualified professional. C 2024-07-31 2024-08-19
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-07-31 2024-09-01
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-07-31 2024-09-05
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-07-31 2024-09-05
0728 Ensure that nurse aides who have worked more than 4 months, are trained and competent; and nurse aides who have worked less than 4 months are enrolled in appropriate training. E 2024-07-31 2024-09-15
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. B 2024-07-31 2024-09-05
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2024-07-31 2024-09-02
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-07-31 2024-09-02
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2024-07-31 2024-09-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-07-31 2024-09-02
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. E 2024-07-31 2024-08-20
0941 Develop, implement, and/or maintain an effective training program that includes effective communications for direct care staff members. B 2024-07-31 2024-09-05
0944 Conduct mandatory training, for all staff, on the facility’s Quality Assurance and Performance Improvement Program. B 2024-07-31 2024-09-05
0949 Provide behavior health training consistent with the requirements and as determined by a facility assessment. B 2024-07-31 2024-09-05
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2023-12-15 2023-12-20
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. E 2023-06-08 2023-07-07
0567 Honor the resident's right to manage his or her financial affairs. E 2023-06-08 2023-07-07

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
